arts.ttl: Live updates: Reading look to continue play-off fight at bottom side Shrewsbury
arts.sum: Reading are looking to keep up their push for the play-offs as they travel to bottom side Shrewsbury Town this evening. Yet to win against the Shrews since returning to League One, Salop are 13 points adrift at the foot of the division.

arts.ttl: Reading team news: Captain comes in from the start in Shrewsbury Town change
arts.sum: Reading captain Andy Yiadom has been drafted in to the starting side against Shrewsbury Town, the only change made by manager Noel Hunt. The Royals need to win in Shropshire at the home of the division's bottom club to keep pace with the play-off race with six matches remaining. Drawing 1-1 in the reverse fixture under Gareth Ainsworth, Michael Appleton has since been drafted in following the departure to Gillingham. Joel Pereira is looking for a second clean sheet in four days after Saturday's 1-0 win over Wycombe Wanderers.

arts.ttl: Dave Edwards: Shrewsbury Town in a painful but necessary process
arts.sum: It's not necessarily an easy watch at the moment, it's not an exciting watch either, but you've got to remember that Michael Appleton's had no time to work with the players and he's trying to overhaul the style of play. But there are some small glimmers of hope from watching the game back and watching how Michael Appleton's trying to play football.

arts.ttl: Peterborough United chief slams 'declining' officiating standards after Reading win
arts.sum: Peterborough United boss Darragh MacAnthony slated referee Adam Herczeg for his performance in Reading's 3-1 victory last week, admitting he had 'never seen a performance like it in 19 years.' The Royals were in scintillating form to complete a first ever league double over Posh in what was an excellent showing of attacking football from both sides. While not knocking the hosts, MacAnthony was scathing about referee Herczeg, particularly for his decision not to award a penalty when Charlie Savage took out Ricky Jade-Jones in the box, which looked like a spot kick from almost anywhere else in the SCL Stadium.

arts.ttl: Rumoured Reading target bags a brace as Newcastle United hit seven at St James Park
arts.sum: Former rumoured Reading target, Garang Kuol netted a brace for Newcastle United Under-21s as the Magpies thrashed the Royals 7-1 at St James' Park. The North-East outfit qualified for the PL2 play-offs with a game to spare following victory, matching the great success being seen at senior level as Eddie Howe's side won the Carabao Cup in March. The Royals, under Peter Scott, found the net through Malta international Basil Tuma in the first half, but were blown away by the hosts. Kuol, who was linked with the club in 2023, has represented Australia at international level and joined Hearts on loan instead of making a move to Berkshire. His brace made it seven for the Under-21 campaign to date.

arts.ttl: 'F***ing morons: Former Reading star on Dai Yongge and possible suspension
arts.sum: Former Reading striker Simon Church has hit out at the English Football League's handling of the club's off-field situation. Wales international Church rose through the youth ranks with the club and scored 24 goals in 122 appearances between 2009 and 2012- scoring seven times as the club were promoted to the Premier League in 2011/12. A former teammate of Noel Hunt and Mikele Leigertwood's- and playing under Nigel Gibbs while with the club- Church spoke passionately about the situation facing the club. Owner Dai Yongge was disqualified as an owner in February and has been given until April 22 to sell the club or face suspension from the league. This comes at the end of a near two-year crisis embroiling the club and seven years into Yongge's reign.

arts.ttl: Reading boss calls for right mentality against Shrewsbury side 'fighting for futures'
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt insists his players will need the right mentality to beat League Two-bound Shrewsbury Town, with many players fighting for their futures. The Shrews are sat rock-bottom of League One and are 12 points adrift of safety with 18 points up for grabs. The Royals, meanwhile, sit seventh and are a point outside the play-offs with six matches remaining, starting at Croud Meadow this evening. "We have been really good in the last two or three months, putting a lovely run together," Hunt said after Saturday's 1-0 win over Wycombe Wanderers. "How we played today, we showed we’re not just a good football team and we can mix it. Different games need different things.

arts.ttl: Reading boss issues injury update on Chelsea youngster ahead of Shrewsbury Town trip
arts.sum: Reading star Ben Elliott is a week away from re-joining full training ahead of a return to first team action. The 22-year-old has two goals and four assists in 19 appearances to date this season but has been missing since December with a thigh injury. While the Chelsea youngster has been missing, the midfield trio of Lewis Wing, Charlie Savage and Harvey Knibbs has developed into one of the most potent in League One. However, the guile and ball carrying ability of Elliott would certainly help push the Royals on for their play-off push, sitting one point outside the top six with six matches remaining.

arts.ttl: Sheffield Wednesday owner makes Reading embargo claim as players are paid late
arts.sum: Sheffield Wednesday owner Dejphon Chansiri used Reading as an example in a recent interview after failing to pay his players on time. The unpopular Thai has owned the Owls since 2015 and spent heavily but has seen the club bounce between the Championship and League One after two unsuccessful play-off campaigns. Due to pay staff and players on Friday, the squad were not paid until Monday, averting a three-window transfer embargo by one day. Clubs are punished if staff are paid late by 20 days in the space of a calendar year via a fine, while this becomes a transfer ban after 30 days.

arts.ttl: Northampton Town hand Reading additional tickets after away-day sell-out
arts.sum: Reading have been given an additional batch of tickets for Saturday's trip to Northampton Town after selling out their original allocation. The Royals are pressing hard for a play-off finish despite the constant fear of EFL suspension due to the off-field crisis engulfing the club. Sat seventh with seven matches remaining and only a point behind sixth-place, Reading are travelling to bottom club Shrewsbury Town tomorrow (Tuesday) before heading to Sixfields on Saturday. Just under 1,500 tickets were snapped up for the match on Friday, but the club have confirmed that the Cobblers have awarded 78 additional tickets, which will go on sale to supporters from 10am on Tuesday.
